{"notes": [{"url": "https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/rlhf", "title": "What Is Reinforcement Learning From Human Feedback (RLHF)?", "summary": "The latest AI trends, brought to you by experts Get curated insights on the most important—and intriguing—AI news. Subscribe to our weekly Think newsletter. Reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) is a machine learning technique in which a “reward model” is trained with direct human feedback, then used to optimize the performance of an artificial intelligence agent through reinforcement learning.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["RLHF, also called reinforcement learning from human preferences, is uniquely suited for tasks with goals that are complex, ill-defined or difficult to specify.", "For example, it would be impractical (or even impossible) for an algorithmic solution to define “funny” in mathematical terms—but easy for humans to rate jokes generated by a large language model (LLM).", "Perhaps most importantly, OpenAI’s 2017 paper noted that its methodology—particularly the introduction of the proximal policy optimization (PPO) algorithm for updating model weights—greatly reduced the cost of gathering and distilling the necessary h", "The first release of code detailing the use of RLHF on language models came from in 2019 from OpenAI4, who went on to release the RLHF-trained InstructGPT in early 2022.5 This was a crucial step in bridging the gap between GPT-3 and the GPT-3.5-turbo", "RLHF has since been used in the training of state-of-the-art LLMs from OpenAI, DeepMind, Google6 and Anthropic.7 Get curated insights on the most important—and intriguing—AI news."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: RLHF, also called reinforcement learning from human preferences, is uniquely suited for tasks with goals that are comple"}, {"url": "https://arxiv.org/pdf/2312.14925", "title": "[PDF] A Survey of Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback - arXiv", "summary": "Building on prior work on the related setting of preference-based reinforcement learning (PbRL), it stands at the intersection of artificial intelligence and human-computer inter- action. This positioning provides a promising approach to enhance the performance and adaptability of intelligent systems while also improving the alignment of their objectives with human values. The success in training large language models (LLMs) has impressively demonstrated this potential in recent years, where RLHF has played a decisive role in direct- ing the model’s capabilities towards human objectives.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Moreover, sparse success signals are not well suited for agent learning, often necessitating reward shaping (Ng et al., 1999), where the reward signal is transformed into one that is more suitable for learning.", "This can lead to reward hacking (Skalse et al., 2022), where agents exploit such correlations to maximize rewards while producing undesired outcomes.", "RLHF has seen a number of successful applications, methodological advances, and theoretical insights since the last comparable survey (Wirth et al., 2017).", "The applications span various domains, including large language model (LLM) fine-tuning (OpenAI, 2022), image generation (Lee et al., 2023), music genera- tion (Cideron et al., 2024), continuous control (Christiano et al., 2017), games (Ibarz et al.,", "In the remainder of this section, we begin by examining the motivation (Section 1.1) and origins (Section 1.2) of RLHF, defining the scope of this survey (Section 1.3), and outlining the structure of subsequent sections (Section 1.4). 1.1 Why Human F"], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Moreover, sparse success signals are not well suited for agent learning, often necessitating reward shaping (Ng et al., "}, {"url": "https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3743127", "title": "RLHF Deciphered: A Critical Analysis of Reinforcement Learning ...", "summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT", "perspectives": [], "key_facts": [], "knowledge_contribution": ""}, {"url": "https://huggingface.co/blog/rlhf", "title": "Illustrating Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF)", "summary": "Illustrating Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) This article is also available in Chinese 简体中文. This article has been translated to Chinese 简体中文 and Vietnamese đọc tiếng việt.Language models have shown impressive capabilities in the past few years by generating diverse and compelling text from human input prompts. However, what makes a "good" text is inherently hard to define as it is subjective and context dependent.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["There are many applications such as writing stories where you want creativity, pieces of informative text which should be truthful, or code snippets that we want to be executable.", "To compensate for the shortcomings of the loss itself people define metrics that are designed to better capture human preferences such as BLEU or ROUGE.", "Wouldn't it be great if we use human feedback for generated text as a measure of performance or go even one step further and use that feedback as a loss to optimize the model?", "This initial model can also be fine-tuned on additional text or conditions, but does not necessarily need to be.", "The underlying goal is to get a model or system that takes in a sequence of text, and returns a scalar reward which should numerically represent the human preference."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: There are many applications such as writing stories where you want creativity, pieces of informative text which should b"}, {"url": "https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s13347-025-00861-0", "title": "Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback in LLMs", "summary": "Vol.:(0123456789) Philosophy & Technology (2025) 38:35 https://doi.org/10.1007/s13347-025-00861-0 RESEARCH ARTICLE Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback in LLMs: Whose Culture, Whose Values, Whose Perspectives? Kristian González Barman1 · Simon Lohse2,3,4 · Henk W. de Regt2 Received: 2 July 2024 / Accepted: 20 February 2025 / Published online: 19 March 2025 © The Author(s) 2025 Abstract We argue for the epistemic and ethical advantages of pluralism in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) in the context of Large Language Models (LLMs). Drawing on social epistemology and pluralist philosophy of science, we suggest ways in which RHLF can be made more responsive to human needs and how we can address challenges along the way.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Vol.:(0123456789) Philosophy & Technology (2025) 38:35 https://doi.org/10.1007/s13347-025-00861-0 RESEARCH ARTICLE Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback in LLMs: Whose Culture, Whose Values, Whose Perspectives?", "Kristian González Barman1 · Simon Lohse2,3,4 · Henk W. de Regt2 Received: 2 July 2024 / Accepted: 20 February 2025 / Published online: 19 March 2025 © The Author(s) 2025 Abstract We argue for the epistemic and ethical advantages of pluralism in Reinf", "This approach is particularly useful when designing AI systems for tasks where it is dif- ficult to specify a precise reward function or when it is important to align the mod- el’s behaviour with certain human expectations and values.", "For instance, RLHF has notably improved language models for context-aware text generation (Ziegler et al., 2020) and taught robots to navigate cluttered environments (Henry et al., 2010).", "RLHF is commonly employed in the later stages of fine-tuning models, particu- larly in the development of prominent Large Language Models (LLMs) like GPT-3.5 or GPT-4."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Vol.:(0123456789) Philosophy & Technology (2025) 38:35 https://doi.org/10.1007/s13347-025-00861-0 RESEARCH ARTICLE Reinf"}, {"url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=McaI5kkQySU", "title": "791: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF)", "summary": "The latest machine learning, A.I., and data career topics from across both academia and industry are brought to you by host Dr. Jon Krohn on Super Data Science, the most listened-to podcast in the industry. In lighthearted conversation with renowned guests, Jon cuts through hype to fuel your professional impact.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Whether you're curious about getting started in a data career or you're a deep technical expert, whether you'd like to understand what A.I. is or you'd like to integrate more data-driven processes into your business, we have inspiring guests and ligh", "A 300-400 word deep analysis focused on findings relevant to the research question, including:
   - Key data points, statistics, and numerical findings
   - Methodology or approach described (if any)
   - Contradictions or tensions with commonly know", "Which STORM perspectives are covered (from: Scientific, Regulatory, Industry, Economic, Public_Health, Historical, Regional, Methodological, Emerging_Trends)
3. 3-5 specific facts with numbers, dates, or named entities
4.", "Any data tables, statistics, or quantitative results (preserve exact numbers)
5."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Whether you're curious about getting started in a data career or you're a deep technical expert, whether you'd like to u"}]}
